'''Darnell McIntosh''' (born 5 July 1997) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce|wg}}er or {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Hull F.C.]] and the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the Super League. He has spent time on loan or dual registration at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
McIntosh was born in [[Huddersfield]], West Yorkshire, England. He is of Grenadian heritage.

McIntosh is a product of the Giants' scholarship system<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.giantsrl.com/locker-room/profile/378/darnell-mcintosh|title = Huddersfield Giants - Rugby Super League}}</ref> and was called up to the [[England Knights]] squad along with 3 other Giants players in 2019.<ref>https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants/huddersfield-giants-quartet-are-selected-for-england-knights-squad-1-9674104 {{Dead link|date=February 2022}}</ref>

==Career==
===Huddersfield Giants===
McIntosh spent time on loan at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in 2016.<ref name="Oldham 'hopeful' over new additions">{{cite web|url=http://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/11/roughyeds-news/98980/oldham-hopeful-over-new-additions|title=Oldham 'hopeful' over new additions|publisher=Oldham Evening Chronicle|date=2016-07-20|accessdate=2016-09-12}}</ref>

In round 3 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored two tries for Huddersfield in a 25-24 loss against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56734758 |title=Hull Kingston Rovers 25-24 Huddersfield Giants - Hull KR score seven-second try |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
In round 11 of the 2022 Super League season, he scored a [[hat-trick]] in Hull F.C.'s 48-12 win over [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61245703/|title=Super League: Hull FC 48-12 Toulouse - Hat-tricks for Darnell McIntosh & Connor Wynne|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

In round 1 of the 2023 Super League season, McIntosh scored two tries for Hull F.C. against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] which ended in a 32–30 victory.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64668525|title=Super League: Hull FC 32-30 Castleford Tigers - Away side fall just short of remarkable fightback|work=BBC Sport }}</ref> He played a total of twenty-one games for Hull F.C. for in the [[2023 Super League season]] and scored ten tries as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=4 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Leigh Leopards===
On 23 April 2024, it was confirmed that McIntosh had signed for [[Leigh Leopards]] in a player swap deal that saw Hull F.C. receive [[Tom Briscoe]] in return.<ref>{{cite news|last=Walker |first=Callum |url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-fc-and-leigh-leopards-complete-player-swap-deal/ |title=Hull FC and Leigh Leopards complete player swap deal |work=Total Rugby League|date=23 April 2024 |access-date=23 April 2024}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
On 13 October 2025, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of McIntosh on a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=13 October 2025 |title=Tigers bring in winger McIntosh from Leigh |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c0l78nzzjnwo |access-date=26 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=TotalRL|date=13 October 2025|author=Walker|url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-snap-up-leigh-leopards-man-darnell-mcintosh/|title=Castleford Tigers snap up Leigh Leopards man Darnell McIntosh|access-date=26 February 2026|first=Callum}}</ref> He was assigned squad number 4 for the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=26 December 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 2026 squad numbers with new-look top 17 and shocks confirmed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-reveal-2026-squad-numbers-with-new-look-top-17-and-shocks-confirmed |access-date=26 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He made his debut against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[2026 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=7 February 2026 |title=Doncaster 0 Castleford Tigers 14: Not picture perfect but job done for Ryan Carr |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/doncaster-0-castleford-tigers-14-not-picture-perfect-but-job-done-for-ryan-carr-5507339 |access-date=26 February 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> and scored his first try in round 3 against [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Aloia|first=Andrew|date=27 February 2026|title=Castleford beat Giants for first Super League win|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2drzj29nno|access-date=5 March 2026|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
